title: stylocline sonorensis
intro: Stylocline sonorensis, also known as Sonoran stylocline or Sonoran scalebush, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is a shrub-like perennial with small yellow flowers and scale-like leaves, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Stylocline sonorensis has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to provide various health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing.
